p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Stable leaf count; foliage remains turgid with consistent yellow pot orientation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained steady state throughout the 5-day sequence; no significant morphological shifts observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: High resilience; no signs of chlorosis or wilting. Alignment with previous moisture recommendations is successful.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves and one emerging pair; centered in black pot.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The leaf margin dehydration noted in previous reports has stabilized; no further necrosis progression observed since T-5.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Recovery trajectory is positive. The cessation of margin browning suggests the plant has adapted to the current VPD environment.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves present; white rabbit anchor (5cm) remains in position.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The tip necrosis on the larger leaf has remained static since T-5, indicating the stressor is no longer active.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable. The lack of further tissue degradation confirms the plant is currently in a maintenance phase.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Small seedling located near the rim of the black pot (shared with p2).
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Growth is slow but steady; no significant changes in leaf orientation or size over the 5-day period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Appears healthy; no signs of etiolation or desiccation.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil moisture appears consistent across all pots; no signs of surface cracking or fungal blooms.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: White particulate matter (perlite/additive) remains visible on the soil surface of p3 and p2; this is a confirmed outcome of user-applied soil amendments.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a transition from a period of active intervention (visible white amendments) to a period of stabilization where no further rapid changes in plant morphology occurred.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of equilibrium. The previous stress indicators (dehydration/necrosis) have ceased progression, suggesting that the current environmental management is effective.
vision_report.anomalies: None. All visual changes are consistent with previous user-directed care.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit confirms that the garden has moved from a state of acute stress to a stable, maintenance-oriented phase. The plants are showing no signs of further decline, and the soil surface remains consistent with previous amendments. The hardware (sensor) in p2 continues to be monitored visually as per instructions, and the visual evidence suggests the plant is coping well with the current VPD.
